page: Microsoft Azure DS12-1_v2 is a virtual machine instance within the DSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor core operating at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This single-core, single-threaded configuration is paired with 28.0 GB of system memory and 57 GB of local storage. The CPU includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache, with support for AVX and AVX-512F vector extensions.

Benchmark data reveals a significant performance asymmetry. While the instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 28 GB per core, its compute and memory throughput are constrained. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks both score at 1022.08, placing the instance in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Similarly, Geekbench scores of 910.0 for single-core and 935.0 for multi-core, along with a Passmark CPU score of 1443.39, reflect limited processing power. Memory bandwidth is also restricted, with large-block read and write speeds reaching 10.4 GB/sec and 8.15 GB/sec respectively, categorizing its memory performance as weak to poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DS12-1_v2 provides a specialized resource profile that avoids the expense of multi-core allocation when only high memory capacity is required. However, the severe compute bottleneck limits its utility for CPU-bound tasks. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as small database caching, lightweight Redis deployments, or simple background tasks that require a large memory footprint but minimal processing throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure DS12-1_v2 is a specialized virtual machine from the DSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL core clocked at 2.6 GHz, paired with 28.0 GB of RAM and 57 GB of local storage. This configuration yields an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio, but benchmark results indicate significant compute limitations. The instance scores in the bottom 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 910.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 1443.39. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block reads at 10.4 GB/sec. Consequently, the DS12-1_v2 is qualitatively cost-efficient only for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without intensive processing demands. It is best suited for lightweight database caching, Redis operations, and memory-heavy, low-compute background tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS12-1_v2 is a Hyper-V virtual machine featuring 1 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPU, 28 GB of RAM, and 57 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low compute requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS12-1_v2 offers 1 Intel Xeon vCPU, 28 GB RAM, and 57 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads.
tagline: A memory-heavy, single-core virtual machine designed for memory-intensive workloads that require minimal processing power and a small local storage footprint.
bullet_points: Single dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL core at 2.6 GHz
28.0 GB of system memory and 57 GB of local storage
High memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound applications
Constrained CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Best suited for lightweight database caching and Redis deployments
